output:
## Results

**Part 1 — Cycles to drop below 4500 mAh: 106 complete cycles**

- Each cycle multiplies capacity by 0.999 (retains 99.9%)
- After 105 cycles: 4501.39 mAh (still above threshold)
- After 106 cycles: 4496.88 mAh (below 4500 mAh)
- Formula: `n = ⌈ln(0.9) / ln(0.999)⌉ = ⌈105.31⌉ = 106`

**Part 2 — Total hours of use: ≈ 1006.23 hours (~41.9 days)**

Each successive cycle provides slightly less runtime because the capacity shrinks. The total is the geometric series:

$$\text{Total hours} = \frac{1}{500} \sum_{i=0}^{105} 5000 \times 0.999^i = 10 \times \frac{1 - 0.999^{106}}{0.001} \approx 1006.23 \text{ hours}$$

Note this is less than the naive estimate of 106 × 10 = 1060 hours — the ~54-hour difference is entirely due to the compounding capacity loss across cycles.
